structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Approach Temperature
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The approach temperature is the difference between the leaving water temperature and the entering wet-bulb temperature.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Range
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The range is the temperature difference between the entering and leaving water.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Drift Loss
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Drift loss is typically 0.005 to 0.2% of the circulating water flow rate.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Cycles of Concentration
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Cycles of concentration is the ratio of dissolved solids in the circulating water to that in the makeup water.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Water Treatment
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Water treatment is required to control scale, corrosion, and biological growth.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Fan Control
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Fans may be cycled on and off or modulated to maintain the desired leaving water temperature.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Location
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Cooling towers should be located to avoid recirculation of discharge air and to minimize noise impact.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Design Wet-Bulb Temperature
structured_payload_candidate.summary: The design wet-bulb temperature is typically selected based on the 1% annual cumulative frequency of occurrence.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Capacity Rating
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Cooling tower capacity is rated in tons, where 1 ton equals 15,000 Btu/h heat rejection.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Seasonal Shutdown
structured_payload_candidate.summary: At seasonal shutdown, the tower should be drained and cleaned to prevent freezing and biological growth.

structured_payload_candidate.title: Cooling Tower Evaporation Loss
structured_payload_candidate.summary: Evaporation loss is approximately 1% of the circulating water flow for each 10°F of range.
